{{Quote|I was wrong about you being a traitor. It's your second-in-command!|Han Solo, to Silver Fyre|<ref name="TG" />}}
'''Kraaken''' was the Deputy Commander of the [[Aquaris Freeholders/Legends|Aquaris Freeholders]], a notorious [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] and [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] gang, serving as the right-hand man of Freeholder leader [[Silver Fyre/Legends|Silver Fyre]] during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Unbeknownst to Fyre and the rest of the gang, Kraaken was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] asset, and plotted to take control of the Freeholders for the Empire even as Fyre pledged her support to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Kraaken's treachery came to light when he attempted to capture Rebel [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], only to have his plans foiled by Fyre and Rebel leaders [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Kraaken didn't respond to my orders—his aqua-skimmer plunged straight at the demonsquid! And Luke's in there with him!|Silver Fyre|<ref name="TG" />}}
[[File:Kraaken_swenc.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Kraaken, during his attempt to betray Silver Fyre and the Rebels.]]
A member of the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] and [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] gang called the [[Aquaris Freeholders/Legends|Aquaris Freeholders]], by the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] Kraaken had worked his way to the position of Deputy Commander, serving as the right-hand man of Freeholders leader [[Silver Fyre/Legends|Silver Fyre]]. Unbeknownst to Fyre and the rest of the Freeholders, Kraaken was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] asset—this proved to be a source of conflict as the war began to near its height. Kraaken accompanied Fyre to the [[Conference of Uncommitted Worlds]] on [[Kabal/Legends|Kabal]], and although he attempted to arrange an Imperial attack to eliminate Fyre and leave him in charge of the Freeholders, he was unsuccessful in preventing Fyre from pledging her support to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Not long afterward, Fyre welcomed Rebel leaders [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] to visit the Freeholders' aquatic base on [[Aquaris]]. Assigned to keep tabs on their visitors, Kraaken overheard that two Rebel [[Droid/Legends|droids]] that had accompanied them—[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]—held secret data vital to the war effort.<ref name="TG" />

Kraaken passed this news on to Fyre, but secretly plotted to snatch the droids himself and deliver them to the Empire. Knowing that Fyre wanted to earn the Rebels' trust—she had earned Solo's ire in the past during their smuggling days—Kraaken proposed that they take Solo and Skywalker out on a hunting trip, where Kraaken would grab the attention of a dangerous [[demonsquid]] and allow Fyre to swoop in and save the day. Fyre agreed to the plan, which was all an elaborate misdirection: Kraaken [[Stun/Legends|stunned]] Skywalker unconscious and left him to the mercy of the devilsquid, while he rushed back to the base to take the droids. However, Organa uncovered and interrupted Kraaken's treachery, while Fyre and Solo rescued Skywalker and rushed back to the Freeholder base, cutting off Kraaken's escape during a firefight with Organa. Although Kraaken attempted to get back at them by destroying the data, Organa had created a duplicate, and his plot amounted to nothing.<ref name="TG" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Kraaken... you miserable, traitorous creep!|Han Solo|<ref name="TG" />}}
Although he had earned Silver Fyre's trust as a competent right-hand man and a gifted [[Spy/Legends|spy]], Kraaken's real loyalties lied with the Galactic Empire. And while he didn't arouse many suspicions as Fyre's top [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]], Kraaken proved himself to be skilled in deception and possessing a keen mind for plots.<ref name="TG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kraaken appeared in ''[[Traitor's Gambit]]'', a [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strip]] story arc written by [[Archie Goodwin]], illustrated by [[Al Williamson]] and released in [[1982]].<ref name="TG" />
